files.content:
[planning]
sprintSize = 5
currentProfile = "human"
docs_sink = "apps/portfolio/content/docs"
conventionsPaths = [
  "AGENTS.md",
  ".planning/AGENTS.md",
  "apps/portfolio/content/docs/documentation/requirements.mdx",
  "apps/portfolio/content/docs/documentation/planning/state.mdx",
  "apps/portfolio/content/docs/documentation/roadmap.mdx",
]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Docs projects — domains that live in the sink and may or may not have a
# separate repo yet. These are real projects (not just display sections):
# they flesh out ideas, requirements, and implementation in various forms.
# Not always code. When a repo exists it can be added to [[planning.roots]].
#
# content-skill = the GAD skill that generates authored/lore content for this
#   project (e.g. gad-manuscript generates magicborn in-world lore).
# kind = "app" | "platform" | "lore" | "meta"
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[[docs.projects]]
id = "dialogue-forge"
sinkPath = "dialogue-forge"
description = "Dialogue editor app — feature docs, architecture, yarn-spinner integration"
kind = "app"

[[docs.projects]]
id = "documentation"
sinkPath = "documentation"
description = "Cross-cutting monorepo meta-docs — conventions, architecture, design system"
kind = "meta"

[[docs.projects]]
id = "books"
sinkPath = "books"
description = "Book platform — reader, publishing pipeline, EPUB architecture"
kind = "platform"

[[docs.projects]]
id = "listen"
sinkPath = "listen"
description = "Listen platform — audio/music app, routes, player architecture"
kind = "platform"

[[docs.projects]]
id = "blog"
sinkPath = "blog"
description = "Blog platform — publishing, authoring, content pipeline"
kind = "platform"

[[docs.projects]]
id = "editor"
sinkPath = "editor"
description = "Editor app — rich text / document editing"
kind = "app"

[[docs.projects]]
id = "screenshot-annotate"
sinkPath = "screenshot-annotate"
description = "Screenshot annotation tool"
kind = "app"

[[docs.projects]]
id = "magicborn"
sinkPath = "magicborn"
description = "Magicborn fiction project — requirements, world-building, in-world lore"
kind = "lore"
content-skill = "gad-manuscript"

[[docs.projects]]
id = "projects"
sinkPath = "projects"
description = "Portfolio project catalogue — project listing and metadata"
kind = "meta"

[profiles.human]
description = "Default human view."

[profiles.agent]
description = "Agent perspective; defaultJson = true."
defaultJson = true

[[planning.roots]]
id = "global"
path = "."
planningDir = ".planning"
discover = false

[[planning.roots]]
id = "get-anything-done"
path = "vendor/get-anything-done"
planningDir = ".planning"
discover = false

[[planning.roots]]
id = "repo-planner"
path = "vendor/repo-planner"
planningDir = ".planning"
discover = false

[[planning.roots]]
id = "grime-time"
path = "vendor/grime-time-site"
planningDir = ".planning"
discover = false

[[planning.roots]]
id = "repub-builder"
path = "vendor/repub-builder"
planningDir = ".planning"
discover = false

[[planning.roots]]
id = "mb-cli-framework"
path = "vendor/mb-cli-framework"
planningDir = ".planning"
discover = false

[[planning.roots]]
id = "gad-manuscript"
path = "vendor/gad-manuscript"
planningDir = ".planning"
discover = false

<decisions>
  <decision id="02-01">
    <title>Dual provider data plane: local SQLite plus Supabase</title>
    <summary>Shared app data and uploaded assets should move behind a provider contract that keeps local SQLite plus local files as the default development path while adding Supabase as the first hosted backend; do not rely on committed `.db` binaries or Git LFS for normal runtime setup.</summary>
    <impact>Books, Listen, and future shared services can target one backend-neutral contract. Auth/session migration stays separate from the first data/storage phase.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/plans/global-data-01/PLAN.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/task-registry.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/documentation/requirements.mdx" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="02-02">
    <title>Supabase hosted bootstrap is standardized around one linked project plus a private reader bucket</title>
    <summary>The hosted bootstrap path should use one linked Supabase project ref, a private `reader-library` bucket, env-driven Payload Postgres/S3 settings in the repo, and a machine-local Codex MCP registration. Only the database password and the S3 access-key pair stay manual secrets.</summary>
    <impact>Most of the hosted setup can now be repeated from the repo and local CLI state without committing `.db` files or keeping a vague dashboard-only checklist.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/plans/global-data-01/PLAN.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/plans/global-data-01/SUPABASE-BOOTSTRAP.mdx" />
      <file path=".env.example" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="03-01">
    <title>repub-builder becomes a standalone git-installable repo and returns as a vendored submodule</title>
    <summary>`@portfolio/repub-builder` should move out of `packages/` and live in the standalone `MagicbornStudios/repub-builder` repository. This monorepo should consume it through a vendored submodule workspace checkout instead of carrying the package as a normal in-tree directory.</summary>
    <impact>The package can behave like a real external library with git URL installs, while the portfolio repo keeps a pinned vendored checkout and stops coupling package structure to app-repo history.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/plans/global-data-02/PLAN.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/task-registry.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/documentation/requirements.mdx" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="03-02">
    <title>Published book binaries move to storage as immutable versioned artifacts while the manifest stays lightweight</title>
    <summary>Built EPUBs and planning-pack zips should publish into storage as immutable versioned artifacts. Payload tracks the current version, while `public/books/manifest.json` keeps only lightweight metadata plus the current remote URLs needed by the site and reader.</summary>
    <impact>The repo stops treating shipped EPUB/zip binaries as normal commit cargo, checkpoint naming stays explicit, and SSR-facing routes can still render deterministic metadata without keeping heavyweight binaries in git.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/plans/global-data-02/PLAN.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/decisions.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/public/books/manifest.json" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="03-03">
    <title>Public SEO surfaces may be repo-first or Postgres-backed, but storage is only for heavy binaries and private assets</title>
    <summary>Public pages may keep authored copy in repo or fetch it from Postgres on the server, but they must resolve through stable slug and metadata loaders. Supabase storage is for heavy binaries, media, generated bundles, originals, and private uploads rather than as the source of crawlable page copy.</summary>
    <impact>The repo can move heavy files and mutable catalogs off git without turning public content into client-only storage reads or sacrificing server-rendered SEO surfaces.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/plans/global-data-02/STORAGE-POLICY.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/plans/global-data-02/SSR-SEO-MATRIX.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/decisions.mdx" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="03-04">
    <title>Public site media uses a manifest bridge during migration instead of forcing immediate content rewrites</title>
    <summary>Heavy public site media should publish into `site-media-assets`, while a small manifest maps legacy `/images/...` paths to storage-backed URLs. Content loaders resolve those paths at runtime so authored MDX and frontmatter can keep stable references during the migration.</summary>
    <impact>The portfolio app can adopt storage-backed media without rewriting every existing document at once, and the same bridge can be extended to other public-media classes until a fuller DB-backed content model is worth the churn.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/plans/global-data-02/PLAN.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/decisions.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/public/media/manifest.json" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="03-05">
    <title>Public catalogs move metadata first, while heavy files move to storage and long-form copy stays repo-first until proven otherwise</title>
    <summary>The next public-catalog migration should separate metadata from heavyweight files. `/apps`, `/projects`, and `/resumes` may move ordering, summaries, links, and file relationships into Postgres collections such as `site-app-records`, `project-records`, and `resume-records`, while downloadable or media-heavy files move into storage collections such as `site-download-assets`. Long-form editorial bodies can stay repo-first until moving them clearly reduces maintenance.</summary>
    <impact>The repo can shed hard-coded arrays, local file scans, and heavy downloadable artifacts without weakening SSR or forcing all public writing into the database before that trade actually pays off.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/plans/global-data-03/PLAN.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/plans/global-data-02/STORAGE-POLICY.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/decisions.mdx" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="03-06">
    <title>Public auth UI remains owner-only in v1</title>
    <summary>The shell/sidebar login refresh should reuse the existing seeded owner account and auth/session routes. Public visitors browse almost everything anonymously, while login only unlocks private and admin surfaces for the single owner configured by the auth env vars until a broader role model is intentionally added.</summary>
    <impact>The UI can adopt shadcn-compatible login and account affordances without implying public signup or multi-user behavior that the backend does not support yet.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/decisions.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/task-registry.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/lib/auth/config.ts" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-01">
    <title>GAD is the universal planning framework — agent-agnostic, not Claude Code-specific</title>
    <summary>GAD (get-anything-done) is the single planning framework for all projects in this repo. Skills are methodology documents (SKILL.md) stored in vendor/get-anything-done/skills/ — they must work with any AI coding assistant, not just Claude Code. Claude Code-specific install paths (~/.claude/) are implementation details, not the canonical source.</summary>
    <impact>All projects register under GAD via planning-config.toml. rp-* skills are deprecated; gad:* skills are canonical. No new framework-specific skill systems are added.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/skills/README.md" />
      <file path=".planning/planning-config.toml" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-02">
    <title>Evals capture actual content, not just byte counts — token counts are the primary efficiency metric</title>
    <summary>Every eval RUN.md must contain verbatim CLI and raw workflow outputs, a unit-by-unit fidelity table, token counts (chars/4), arithmetic showing all formulas applied, and an agent simulation proving the next-action decision is identical with and without the CLI. Byte counts alone are insufficient because they cannot reveal whether information is actually readable or complete.</summary>
    <impact>All future eval runs follow the scientific format defined in vendor/get-anything-done/evals/DEFINITIONS.md. v3 is the first run that meets this standard.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/DEFINITIONS.md" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/cli-efficiency/REQUIREMENTS.md" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/cli-efficiency/v3/RUN.md" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-03">
    <title>Context = session-variable information units; static docs are exempt from CLI coverage</title>
    <summary>Context for agent resumption is defined as the 10 session-variable information units (U1–U10) in DEFINITIONS.md. Static docs (AGENTS.md style guide, planning model reference tables, done-task history) do not constitute context loss if absent from CLI output — they are read once and cached. Context loss occurs only when a unit's absence would cause an agent to make a different next-action decision.</summary>
    <impact>Completeness scoring is now honest: units present at reduced fidelity (Truncated/Approximated) score 0.5, not 1.0. v3 completeness = 0.85, lower than v2's inflated 0.95.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/DEFINITIONS.md" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-04">
    <title>All session intents, decisions, and guidance must be captured in planning docs each session</title>
    <summary>Every working session that produces new decisions, requirements, or guidance must update DECISIONS.xml (root) and the relevant section planning docs before the session ends. Chat context is ephemeral — planning docs are the durable record. The GAD planning loop is the mechanism; this decision makes the capture requirement explicit.</summary>
    <impact>Agents starting a new session get current decisions from the planning docs, not from reconstructing chat history. Drift between chat intent and planning state is prevented.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path=".planning/DECISIONS.xml" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/DEFINITIONS.md" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-05">
    <title>repo-planner is officially a GAD project; rp-* skills are deprecated</title>
    <summary>vendor/repo-planner is registered as a GAD root (id=repo-planner) in planning-config.toml. It has a GAD-format .planning/ directory. Its skills (rp-*) are deprecated stubs pointing to gad:* equivalents. The manuscript skill content migrated to vendor/get-anything-done/skills/manuscript/SKILL.md. The portfolio docs (apps/portfolio/content/docs/repo-planner/planning/) remain as the docs sink.</summary>
    <impact>gad projects list shows both global and repo-planner. gad state, phases, and tasks all work for repo-planner. No new rp-* skills should be created.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path=".planning/planning-config.toml" />
      <file path="vendor/repo-planner/.planning/STATE.md" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/skills/README.md" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="03-07">
    <title>Phase-one owner account UI reads avatar data from the shared user record and session payload</title>
    <summary>The first owner-shell pass should not stop at initials-only identity. The shared user model and `/api/auth/session` payload should carry an avatar URL so the sidebar can render a SaaS-style account footer immediately, while still falling back to initials when no avatar exists.</summary>
    <impact>The owner shell now exposes a real account card without coupling the UI to Supabase-specific helpers, and future org-user work can reuse the same session contract.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/decisions.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/lib/auth/viewer.ts" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/lib/auth/permissions.ts" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/components/auth/SidebarAccountSection.tsx" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="03-08">
    <title>The first mounted Payload admin pass stays focused on owner-managed collections and hides internal maintenance tables</title>
    <summary>The upstream `(payload)` route group should own `/admin`, but the first customization pass should only foreground collections that behave like operational content surfaces. Internal reader-state and RAG maintenance collections stay hidden until their workflows are intentionally designed, while public-read asset collections keep public read access and gain owner write access through Payload.</summary>
    <impact>The mounted admin feels like a usable owner back office immediately instead of exposing every internal record table, and the existing runtime/public asset flows keep working while Payload becomes the editing surface for the collections that belong there.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/content/docs/global/planning/decisions.mdx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/app/(payload)/admin/[[...segments]]/page.tsx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/lib/payload/collections/siteAppRecords.ts" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/lib/payload/collections/ragSources.ts" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="05-01">
    <title>Cross-project observability stays lightweight: public Vercel Analytics plus request-id tracing on sensitive server routes</title>
    <summary>The portfolio and Grime Time public layouts should use Vercel Analytics for cheap pageview insight, but analytics events must strip query strings and fragments, ignore internal or admin paths, and avoid emails or other PII. Server-side observability for this pass stays small: generate request ids, return them in response headers, and log safe route summaries plus timings for high-signal Grime Time routes instead of dumping raw request payloads.</summary>
    <impact>The repo gets usable traffic and route-level debugging signals quickly without committing to a full observability platform or leaking sensitive form and copilot data into analytics or ad hoc logs.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="apps/portfolio/app/layout.tsx" />
      <file path="apps/portfolio/lib/analytics.ts" />
      <file path="vendor/grime-time-site/src/app/(frontend)/layout.tsx" />
      <file path="vendor/grime-time-site/src/lib/observability.ts" />
    </references>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-06">
    <title>GAD CLI is format-agnostic: XML, MD, MDX all valid in .planning/</title>
    <summary>Sub-repos and .planning/ directories may use XML, MD, or MDX. The GAD CLI must parse all three transparently. The planning sink normalizes to MDX only. compile() converts any format to MDX; decompile() restores the original format. Coding agents and the CLI treat methodology as the constant — format is an implementation detail.</summary>
    <impact>gad sink validate checks parseability and round-trip fidelity, not format compliance. grim-time-site can stay XML. Any new project may choose its format.</impact>
    <references><file path="vendor/get-anything-done/docs/CLI-SPEC.md" /></references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-07">
    <title>gad-manuscript is a self-contained domain translator repo</title>
    <summary>gad-manuscript is not registered in the portfolio planning-config.toml. It is a standalone repo that defines the domain translator pattern — adapts GAD base methodology to book/fiction creation. It owns its own SKILL.md files, evals, and planning docs while referencing gad:* base skills.</summary>
    <impact>Domain translator pattern is now defined. Other verticals (product, design) follow the same pattern: standalone repo, own skills, own evals, references gad:* base.</impact>
    <references><file path="vendor/gad-manuscript/" /></references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-08">
    <title>GAD v1.0.0 defining characteristic: skills-first with evaluation</title>
    <summary>Pre-1.0 (GSD/ralph-wiggum loop) was workflow-heavy — agents followed long workflow docs. v1.0.0 is skills-first: every skill has an agent-agnostic SKILL.md definition, is evaluated for output quality, and is versioned with the methodology. The CLI provides context efficiency. Evals are scientific. Traces measure what agents retrieve. Domain translators extend GAD to verticals.</summary>
    <impact>All new skills must have SKILL.md in vendor/get-anything-done/skills/. Skills are evaluated via the eval framework. Methodology version is tracked in package.json gadMethodologyVersion field.</impact>
    <references><file path="vendor/get-anything-done/skills/README.md" /><file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/DEFINITIONS.md" /></references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-09">
    <title>gad context inlines file contents by default; --refs is the lightweight mode</title>
    <summary>The `gad context` command now inlines all planning file contents directly into its output by default. This gives agents a single command to get full context without reading files separately. The `--refs` flag restores the old lightweight behavior (file paths only). This improves the completeness score on the cli-efficiency eval — agents no longer need to issue secondary read commands after `gad context`.</summary>
    <impact>Agents must use `gad context --refs` when they only want file paths. `gad context` without flags is now verbose — suitable for session start, not for routine orientation checks.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/cli-efficiency/REQUIREMENTS.md" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-10">
    <title>gad snapshot is the one-shot full project context command</title>
    <summary>`gad snapshot` inlines every planning file in a project's .planning/ directory (excluding archive/, sessions/, node_modules/). Priority order: STATE, ROADMAP, REQUIREMENTS, DECISIONS, TASK-REGISTRY, then alphabetical. This is the canonical command for handing an agent a complete cold-start context pack. It differs from `gad context` in scope — context gives session-aware minimal refs, snapshot gives everything.</summary>
    <impact>Agents starting a fresh session on an unfamiliar project use `gad snapshot --project <id>`. `gad context` is for ongoing work within a session.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-11">
    <title>gad sink is the canonical docs sync command; gad docs compile is an alias</title>
    <summary>`gad sink` owns the four-direction docs sync surface: status (diff), compile (planning→docs), decompile (docs→planning), validate (mapping health). `gad docs compile` remains for backward compatibility but is an alias path through the same docs-compiler.cjs library. The sink is manual by default (sink_workflow = "manual") — no auto-compile on file change.</summary>
    <impact>New code uses `gad sink compile` not `gad docs compile`. The docs-compiler.cjs library is shared. `gad sink decompile` is the canonical way to pull docs edits back into planning files.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs" />
      <file path=".planning/planning-config.toml" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-12">
    <title>Missing-arg UX: print available choices and rerun hint, never a blind error</title>
    <summary>All GAD CLI commands that require a `--project` or `--id` argument must, when that argument is absent, print the list of valid choices and a ready-to-run rerun hint on stderr before exiting 1. This applies to: eval run/score/diff (list eval projects), session resume/close (list active sessions). Blind citty "Missing required argument" errors are banned — required: true is replaced with our own check.</summary>
    <impact>Any future command added to gad.cjs that takes a required project/session/id arg must follow this pattern. The listEvalProjectsHint() and listActiveSessionsHint() helpers are the canonical implementation.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-13">
    <title>TRACE.json is the machine-readable sidecar for every eval run</title>
    <summary>Every eval run directory must contain a TRACE.json alongside RUN.md and SCORE.md. TRACE.json records: workflow (A/B/both), per-command token counts and unit fidelity, unit_coverage map (U1–U12 at Full/Referenced/Truncated/Approximated/Absent), and computed totals (tokens, completeness). Agents write it manually using `gad eval trace write` or inline during a run. The trace is the input to `gad eval trace diff/report` for trend analysis across runs.</summary>
    <impact>Any new eval run must produce a TRACE.json. Backfill is done via `gad eval trace write` using data from the RUN.md. The diff command surfaces regressions and improvements per information unit. The report command shows persistent problem units across the full run history of a project.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/DEFINITIONS.md" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/cli-efficiency/v3/TRACE.json" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-14">
    <title>gad state and gad phases default to session project when a session is active</title>
    <summary>When no --projectid flag is given, `gad state` and `gad phases` call `getActiveSessionProjectId()` which reads the most-recent non-closed session JSON and returns its projectId. If found, output is scoped to that project only. If the projectId doesn't match any registered root (shouldn't happen), falls back to all projects. Explicit `--projectid` overrides the session default. This drops CLI tokens from 2,341 (v5, 3 projects) to 1,104 (v6, 1 project) while keeping completeness = 1.000. token_reduction: 0.874 → 0.940. Composite: 0.950 → 0.976.</summary>
    <impact>Agents working in a session see only their project state by default — no extra flag. Multi-project output still available via explicit --projectid or when no session exists. All three eval targets hit simultaneously for the first time in v6.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/cli-efficiency/v6/SCORE.md" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/cli-efficiency/v6/TRACE.json" />
    </references>
  </decision>
  <decision id="gad-15">
    <title>portfolio-bare eval uses real agent sessions, not a harness — context_mode is self-declared</title>
    <summary>The portfolio-bare eval is not an automated harness. It is a real agent session in a template directory (evals/portfolio-bare/template/) that contains only planning docs and requirements — no app code. The agent opens the template in Claude Code or Codex, runs `gad session new [--fresh]` to declare context mode, plans phases from requirements, executes tasks, and writes a trace at the end. context_mode (fresh|loaded) is stored in the session JSON and stamped on each task in TASK-REGISTRY.xml. context_delta emerges from comparing runs across context modes over time. The user is responsible for declaring the correct context mode. Skills: `gad:eval-run` (running the eval), `gad:eval-report` (comparing runs).</summary>
    <impact>No API keys, no execSync harness. The eval IS real agent work. The report (`gad eval trace report --project portfolio-bare`) shows which tasks are context-dependent vs requirements-derivable. A task that only appears in loaded runs is one the agent misses when starting cold — that is the most actionable finding.</impact>
    <references>
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/portfolio-bare/template/" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/skills/eval-run/SKILL.md" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/skills/eval-report/SKILL.md" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/evals/portfolio-bare/gad.json" />
      <file path="vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad.cjs" />
    </references>
  </decision>
</decisions>

files.content:
# Repo Planner - Getting started

You do not have to use this monorepo's layout to benefit from a planning loop: keep visible requirements, a roadmap, a state pointer, and an ordered task registry. Agents and humans read the same files; ids stay stable in chat and commits.

In this repo, the **Global** docs section is the human-first workflow surface. RepoPlanner's job is to summarize and accelerate that loop, not replace it with a parallel planning model.

## Reference implementation

[MagicbornStudios/RepoPlanner](https://github.com/MagicbornStudios/RepoPlanner) provides:

- XML-shaped templates under `.planning/templates/` when you run **full** init.
- The `repo-planner` CLI entrypoint backed by `loop-cli.mjs` for snapshot, task updates, reports, `setup checklist`, and greenfield bootstrap.
- An embeddable cockpit UI for Next hosts. Policy for this site lives in [decisions](/docs/repo-planner/planning/decisions).

## Submodule

| Path | Upstream |
| --- | --- |
| `vendor/repo-planner` | [MagicbornStudios/RepoPlanner](https://github.com/MagicbornStudios/RepoPlanner) |

Update intentionally: `cd vendor/repo-planner && git fetch && git checkout <ref>` then commit the submodule pointer from the host repo.

```bash
git submodule update --init --recursive vendor/repo-planner
```

## Root scripts

Defined in the repository root `package.json`:

| Script | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `pnpm planning` | `scripts/run-planning-cli.mjs` -> `repo-planner` CLI, with `REPOPLANNER_REPORTS_DIR=.planning-reports` by default |
| `pnpm planning:init` | `init --minimal --no-agents-md` - `.planning/` = XML + `planning-config.toml` + `.planning/AGENTS.md`; may create repo-root `REQUIREMENTS.md` stub if missing; canonical narrative = [documentation requirements](/docs/documentation/requirements) + [documentation state](/docs/documentation/planning/state); no `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`; no `.planning/templates`, `phases/`, `reports/`, or narrative `.md` under `.planning/` |
| `pnpm planning:init:force` | Same with `--force` |
| `pnpm planning:init:full` | Upstream-style init with templates and phase folders for cases where you need the full bootstrap locally |
| `pnpm planning:init:full:force` | Full init with `--force` |
| `pnpm planning:snapshot` | Snapshot / agent context |
| `pnpm planning:checklist` | `setup checklist` |
| `pnpm planning:ui` / `pnpm planning:report-view` | Local report viewer |
| `pnpm planning:embed-packs` | `planning pack embed-build` for `apps/portfolio/public/planning-embed/builtin-packs.json` |
| `pnpm planning -- --version` | Prints the vendored package version |

CLI dependencies (`commander`, `fast-xml-parser`, `@iarna/toml`, `ejs`, `gray-matter`) are installed at the workspace root so Node can resolve them when executing the vendored script.

## Formal CLI entrypoints

RepoPlanner now ships a package bin named `repo-planner`.

Use these surfaces intentionally:

- `pnpm planning ...` is the default operator command in this monorepo because it injects the reports-dir default and always targets this checkout.
- `pnpm exec repo-planner ...` works anywhere the vendored package is installed as a dependency.
- `pnpm dlx repo-planner ...` and global installs are the intended publish surface once the package is published outside this repo; they should use the same bin entry and command help as the local wrapper.

## Publish story

RepoPlanner now carries a concrete publish contract instead of hand-waving around "later":

| Topic | Contract |
| --- | --- |
| package name | `repo-planner` |
| artifact style | source-first package; hosts transpile package source instead of expecting a precompiled JS bundle |
| peer ownership | React, Next, and UI runtime packages stay in `peerDependencies` so the host controls those versions |
| bootstrap assets | `.planning/` ships in the tarball because CLI init depends on those assets |
| version cadence | patch = bug/docs/package fix; minor = additive exports/commands; major = breaking CLI or host-contract change |

Verification for this phase is a package tarball dry-run from `vendor/repo-planner`:

```bash
pnpm pack --dry-run
```

## Consumer contract

When another app embeds RepoPlanner, the required contract should be explicit:

| Concern | Expectation |
| --- | --- |
| `Next.js` | add `repo-planner` to `transpilePackages` |
| `Tailwind` | include vendored or installed RepoPlanner source in content scanning |
| `CSS` | import `repo-planner/planning.css` once |
| `Imports` | use package subpaths such as `repo-planner`, `repo-planner/host`, `repo-planner/planning-pack`, and `repo-planner/cockpit-host-context` |
| `Utilities` | RepoPlanner ships its own `cn` helper and package-local primitives; hosts should not rely on `@/vendor/repo-planner` or app-local utility aliases for package internals |
| `Host APIs` | provide state / metrics / latest-report routes and point them at the configured planning root |

The vendored upstream docs for this live in [INSTALL.md](https://github.com/MagicbornStudios/RepoPlanner/blob/main/INSTALL.md).

### Route installer

RepoPlanner also ships `vendor/repo-planner/scripts/install-routes.mjs`.

Use it against a Next app root, not a random package folder:

```bash
node vendor/repo-planner/scripts/install-routes.mjs --app-dir=apps/portfolio --dry-run
```

The installer now writes package-import re-export routes such as `repo-planner/api/planning-state/route`, understands the `apps/portfolio` layout, and defaults to read-only GET routes. Only opt into `planning-cli/run` or `planning-edits/apply` when the host explicitly wants mutable/browser-triggered operations.

```bash
node vendor/repo-planner/scripts/install-routes.mjs \
  --app-dir=apps/portfolio \
  --include-cli-run \
  --include-edits-apply
```

### Quick commands

```bash
pnpm planning setup checklist
pnpm planning snapshot
pnpm exec repo-planner --help
```

Pass `--root <path>` or set `REPOPLANNER_PROJECT_ROOT` if the CLI must resolve a different project root than `process.cwd()`. Set `REPOPLANNER_REPORTS_DIR` (relative to project root or absolute) for `usage.jsonl` / `latest.md`; this monorepo defaults to `.planning-reports/` via `scripts/run-planning-cli.mjs` and `next.config.ts`. See [decisions](/docs/repo-planner/planning/decisions) `REPOPLAN-REPORTS-01`.

## Minimal bootstrap

```bash
pnpm planning:init
```

Creates `.planning/` for XML + `planning-config.toml` + `.planning/AGENTS.md`. Execution lives in `ROADMAP.xml` / `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` / `STATE.xml`, not `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`. `REQUIREMENTS.xml` is a stub that points at the documentation-first narrative sources.

## MDX section docs vs XML

- Public narrative + section registries: `apps/portfolio/content/docs/<section>/planning/*.mdx` for planning records, with non-planning reference docs beside that folder.
- Structured agent loop (RepoPlanner): `.planning/*.xml` + `planning-config.toml` after `pnpm planning:init`.
- Monorepo narrative + cross-cutting queue: [documentation requirements](/docs/documentation/requirements), [documentation state](/docs/documentation/planning/state), and [documentation roadmap](/docs/documentation/roadmap).

## Planning files: what to use when

| File | Role |
| --- | --- |
| `ROADMAP.xml` | Product / theme timeline |
| `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` | Tasks, verification commands, status |
| `STATE.xml` | Current pointer: `current-phase`, `next-action`, optional agent registry |
| [documentation requirements](/docs/documentation/requirements) + [documentation state](/docs/documentation/planning/state) + [documentation roadmap](/docs/documentation/roadmap) | Canonical monorepo narrative and cross-cutting queue |
| `.planning-reports/` | Local-only CLI usage and generated reports |

`phases/` exists only after `pnpm planning:init:full`. The default minimal layout has no `.planning/phases/`.

## Target workflow support

RepoPlanner is being shaped to help agents do four things faster:

| Need | Target support |
| --- | --- |
| Pick a phase | Snapshot surfaces active phases, recommended next phase(s), percent complete, effort, dependencies, missing DoD fields, missing tests, orphaned tasks, and stale state. Inputs come only from config-declared planning roots. |
| Start a vague phase | Kickoff scaffolding runs when a phase is vague, stale, or over the host's estimated-hours threshold. |
| Close a phase correctly | Done gate checks tests, verification commands, and planning updates. Executable work is not done without tests. |
| Place work correctly | Ownership guidance explains when work belongs in Global/root planning versus a section planner. |

Concrete behavior for those surfaces lives in phase `repo-planner-integration-05`.

## Snapshot and cockpit shape

Recommended workflow surface:

| Area | What should show |
| --- | --- |
| `Top recommendation lane` | next best phases with score, why-now reasons, blockers, effort label, ownership target, and direct links |
| `Rules reminder` | compact `AGENTS.md` summary card: read order, kickoff trigger, tests-required-for-done, link to full source |
| `Warnings` | missing tests, missing DoD fields, stale state pointers, stale phases, orphan tasks, blocked dependencies |
| `Main inspector` | structured requirements, roadmap, state, task, decision, and kickoff panels as lists, cards, badges, and tables |

The file tree remains useful because it preserves source-of-truth file boundaries, but the work surface should primarily use parsed data rather than page-style prose rendering.

## Bare `.planning` vs full upstream init

**Contract:** `.planning/` is the root planning section (XML + `planning-config.toml` + `.planning/AGENTS.md`). Human monorepo narrative lives in documentation MDX; `.planning/*.xml` is the machine loop.

**Default `pnpm planning:init`** creates:

| Under `.planning/` | At repo root | Omitted by default |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `planning-config.toml`, `STATE.xml`, `TASK-REGISTRY.xml`, `ROADMAP.xml`, `DECISIONS.xml`, `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml`, `REQUIREMENTS.xml`, `AGENTS.md` | `REQUIREMENTS.md` stub only if bootstrap creates it | `.planning/templates/`, `.planning/phases/`, `.planning/README.md`, `.planning/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`, `.planning/reports/` |

Local-only reports live in `.planning-reports/` (gitignored).

## Standalone cockpit

1. Clone [MagicbornStudios/RepoPlanner](https://github.com/MagicbornStudios/RepoPlanner) or use a release build.
2. Install dependencies for the web app when present.
3. Point it at this repository with `REPOPLANNER_PROJECT_ROOT=/absolute/path/to/custom_portfolio`.
4. Edit in the cockpit, export, and commit from git.

## Embedded cockpit on this site

[`/apps/repo-planner`](/apps/repo-planner) mounts the vendored UI with read-only server defaults: GET routes may read committed `.planning/`; `POST /api/planning-edits/apply` and `POST /api/planning-cli/run` return `403` unless `PLANNING_COCKPIT_ALLOW_SERVER_WRITES=1`. No portfolio-hosted planning LLM. Treat **Pack** and **Live** as explicit, distinct modes so labels and empty states stay honest.

| Piece | Location |
| --- | --- |
| Route | `apps/portfolio/app/apps/repo-planner/page.tsx` |
| Client | `apps/portfolio/components/repo-planner/RepoPlannerCockpitClient.tsx` |
| Package | `apps/portfolio/package.json` -> `"repo-planner": "file:../../vendor/repo-planner"` |
| Transpile + env | `next.config.ts` -> `transpilePackages`, `REPOPLANNER_PROJECT_ROOT`, `REPOPLANNER_REPORTS_DIR` |
| APIs | `app/api/planning-*` + `lib/repo-planner/` |

Pack mode now supports two upload behaviors:

- `Import` creates an editable local pack persisted in browser storage.
- `Preview upload` opens a read-only ephemeral pack JSON or `.planning` zip with no production write path and no saved browser workspace mutation.

# Repo Planner - Decisions

## `REPOPLAN-SHELL-01` -- Cockpit shell lives in the imported package

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | Project switcher, upload pack, export workspace, init-template downloads, and the dashboard chrome around `PlanningCockpit` are owned by the `repo-planner` package and imported into the portfolio. The host app does not keep a long-term parallel shell once the package exports a single host-mountable shell. |
| **Rationale** | One UX surface, one place to fix bugs; portfolio only supplies routes, API handlers, env, and host-specific props. |
| **Consequences** | `repo-planner-integration-03` includes upstream or transitional extraction of that shell; the portfolio page becomes a thin import plus layout wrapper. |

## `REPOPLAN-EMBED-01` -- Public embed is read-only on the server

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | Embedded cockpit at `/apps/repo-planner` stays read-only on the server by default. GET routes may read committed `.planning/`; write routes return `403` unless explicitly enabled by env flag. No portfolio-hosted planning LLM is assumed. |
| **Rationale** | Same spirit as the EPUB reader: browser-local and export-to-git, not casual server mutation. |
| **Consequences** | Operators use `pnpm planning` or standalone RepoPlanner for write-capable workflows. |

## `REPOPLAN-REPORTS-01` -- CLI telemetry lives outside `.planning/`

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | `REPOPLANNER_REPORTS_DIR` defaults to `.planning-reports/` at repo root so usage logs and generated reports do not recreate `.planning/reports/` after minimal init. |
| **Rationale** | Bare `.planning/` stays XML plus config only; reports are local operator artifacts. |

## `REPOPLAN-CLI-01` -- Two CLIs, `dlx`-first publish story

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | Root `pnpm planning ...` remains the monorepo operator wrapper, but RepoPlanner now also exposes a formal `repo-planner` bin from the package itself. Local/file installs should use `pnpm exec repo-planner ...`; published `pnpm dlx repo-planner ...` and global installs should reuse the same entrypoint when the package is published. |
| **Work ids** | `documentation-site-07-01`, `repo-planner-integration-01-13` |

## `REPOPLAN-PUBLISH-01` -- Source-first public package with semver gates

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | RepoPlanner publishes as `repo-planner` with a source-first package contract: runtime source files, CLI scripts, exported subpaths, and `.planning/` bootstrap assets ship in the tarball; React/Next/UI runtime packages remain peer dependencies. |
| **Rationale** | The package is primarily an embeddable host surface plus CLI, so consumers need the source entrypoints and bootstrap assets more than a separate build artifact. Keeping React/Next in peers prevents the package from pinning the host runtime. |
| **Consequences** | Use semver intentionally: patch for docs/package/non-breaking fixes, minor for additive exports or commands, major for export-path or CLI-contract breaks. Verify with `pnpm pack --dry-run` before calling the publish story done. |

## `REPOPLAN-ROUTES-01` -- Hand-maintained Next API routes

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | RepoPlanner's route installer now targets package subpath imports and monorepo app roots like `apps/portfolio`, but it stays read-only by default: GET routes only unless the operator explicitly opts into `planning-cli/run` or `planning-edits/apply`. This repo may continue to keep explicit `app/api/planning-*` routes where host-local behavior is clearer than generated stubs. |

## `REPOPLAN-UPLOAD-01` -- Preview uploads stay ephemeral and read-only

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | Pack mode distinguishes between editable local imports and preview uploads. `Import` creates a writable browser-local pack. `Preview upload` accepts pack JSON or `.planning` zip input, opens it read-only, does not persist it into local storage, and does not expose a production write path. |
| **Rationale** | Operators sometimes need to inspect a planning bundle quickly without mutating workspace state or implying a live repo connection. |
| **Consequences** | Tracked by `repo-planner-integration-01-06`; Pack copy and sidebar labels must make the ephemeral/read-only distinction explicit. |

## `REPOPLAN-PACK-MODAL-01` -- Planning pack copy

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | Planning pack modal copy should state that packs align with [RepoPlanner](https://github.com/MagicbornStudios/RepoPlanner) conventions and link upstream. |

## `REPOPLAN-WORKFLOW-01` -- RepoPlanner accelerates the loop; it does not replace it

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | RepoPlanner should summarize and scaffold the repo's planning loop: Global docs stay the human-first entrypoint, while the CLI and cockpit help agents pick phases faster, create kickoff records, surface missing definition-of-done fields, and refuse executable-behavior closeout without tests. |
| **Rationale** | The tool should reduce rereading and bookkeeping while still preserving the roadmap/state/task-registry/decisions model. |
| **Consequences** | Planned work includes workflow-aware snapshot, kickoff scaffolding, done gates, ownership guidance, and cockpit visibility for missing tests or missing DoD data in `repo-planner-integration-05`. |

## `REPOPLAN-MODES-01` -- Pack mode vs Live mode in the embed

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | The portfolio embed exposes two explicit data modes: Pack (built-in gallery plus user uploads) and Live (server reads the mounted repo's `.planning/` via GET APIs). UI copy, empty states, and sidebar labels must not imply a live monorepo connection when the user is in Pack-only mode. |
| **Rationale** | Browser code cannot read arbitrary disk paths; only Live mode justifies "this repository" semantics. |
| **Consequences** | Tracked as `repo-planner-integration-03-08` and `REPOPLAN-REQ-10`. |

## `REPOPLAN-SNAPSHOT-01` -- Snapshot is a steering surface, not a document reader

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | Snapshot output should default to structured steering data: ranked active/planned phases only, why-now reasons, blocker warnings, ownership targets, sprint status, completion percent, effort labels, stale signals, missing-test or missing-DoD warnings, and unresolved phase open questions. When a phase crosses the `needs discussion` threshold, the recommended action should shift from implementation toward discussion. Snapshot should not dump whole planning pages into the main surface. |
| **Rationale** | Agents need the next good action faster than they need prose recall. The file tree already preserves the document model; snapshot should reduce scanning cost. |
| **Consequences** | Tracked by `REPOPLAN-REQ-13`, `repo-planner-integration-05-01`, and `repo-planner-integration-06-04`. |

## `REPOPLAN-AGENTS-01` -- Show AGENTS as a compact rules reminder

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | RepoPlanner should show a compact `AGENTS.md` reminder card in snapshot and cockpit views: read order, **kickoff when vague / stale / estimated hours over policy max** (not for every phase — **`REPOPLAN-KICKOFF-01`**), tests-required-for-done, and a deep link to the full file. Full AGENTS prose stays available as a linked source, not as the default pane content. |
| **Rationale** | Operators need the loop rules visible, but the full document is too dense to inline in every workflow surface. |
| **Consequences** | Tracked by `REPOPLAN-REQ-14` and `repo-planner-integration-05-07`. |

## `REPOPLAN-UI-01` -- File tree for navigation, structured panes for work

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | Keep the file tree as the primary navigation model, but render known planning files as structured cards, lists, tables, badges, forms, sprint views, and percentages in the main pane. Raw markdown or XML stays out of the normal RepoPlanner product surface. |
| **Rationale** | Planning files are good storage and traceability units, but poor day-to-day steering surfaces when rendered as full documents. |
| **Consequences** | Tracked by `REPOPLAN-REQ-15`, `REPOPLAN-REQ-16`, and phase `repo-planner-integration-06`. |

## `REPOPLAN-SCORING-01` -- Weighted recommendation formula stays explicit

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | Use an explicit weighted score to rank candidate phases. Baseline recommendation score = `strategic-importance + unblock-value + urgency + momentum + test-followup-value + recency-boost - shrinking-global-penalty - effort-penalty - blocked-penalty - open-question-entropy penalty`. Missing tests are a positive pickup signal for open executable work, not a recommendation penalty. Created/updated timestamps act as tie-breakers or small boosts only. Each open question contributes equally to entropy, and `needs discussion` lowers implementation recommendation rather than acting as badge-only metadata. The simplified action set stays `Implement now`, `Discuss first`, and `Unblock dependency`; when both blockers and unresolved-question entropy are present, `Discuss first` outranks `Unblock dependency` so the recommendation reflects planning risk instead of pretending the only next step is code. |
| **Rationale** | CLI and cockpit must recommend the same work for the same data, and operators need to understand why one phase outranks another. High question count means high planning entropy, which is a poor random implementation pickup even when the phase is strategically important. |
| **Consequences** | Tracked by `repo-planner-integration-05-01` and `repo-planner-integration-05-06`. |

## `REPOPLAN-KICKOFF-01` -- Effort threshold uses estimated hours

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | **Above effort threshold** means the phase’s **estimated hours** exceed a **host-configured maximum** (policy). **Estimated hours** come from an explicit field on the phase (roadmap / registry row) or, when absent, from **summed task estimates** under that phase where the format supports it. Vague and stale triggers are unchanged. |
| **Rationale** | Hours match scheduling and session planning better than raw task counts. |
| **Consequences** | `repo-planner-integration-05-02`, `repo-planner-integration-05-06`, and schema work document the field names, fallback sum rule, and default cutoff. |

## `REPOPLAN-POLICY-01` -- Portfolio workflow rules are host policy, not invisible upstream defaults

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | RepoPlanner should support host-configured workflow policy. This portfolio can require Global-first read order, **kickoff when a phase is vague, stale, or above estimated-hours threshold** (see **`REPOPLAN-KICKOFF-01`**), and tests-required-for-done, while other projects may choose different rules. Upstream defaults should stay generic and discoverable. |
| **Rationale** | The product needs to stay reusable outside this repo even while this repo drives its design. |
| **Consequences** | Tracked by `REPOPLAN-REQ-18` and `repo-planner-integration-06-06`. |

## `REPOPLAN-FORMS-01` -- Pack editing autosaves locally; IDs are immutable in normal flows

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | RepoPlanner form editing uses debounced client-side autosave with visible save-state feedback. Live mode remains read-only. IDs are immutable in normal editing once created; any rename is an advanced migration action that rewrites references and is unavailable in Live mode. |
| **Rationale** | Operators want low-friction editing, but ids are reference keys and Live mode must stay honest about mutability. |
| **Consequences** | Tracked by `REPOPLAN-REQ-21`, `REPOPLAN-REQ-22`, `repo-planner-integration-06-02`, and `repo-planner-integration-06-07`. |

## `REPOPLAN-QUESTIONS-01` -- Phase open questions stay visible even when non-blocking

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | Every phase may carry visible open questions surfaced by agents during planning or execution. Questions stay phase-scoped, use a single text field, stay in created order, do not block phase start by default, and use only two states: `open` and `answered` (decision recorded). In the UI, answered questions should live in a collapsed history area rather than disappearing. Two or more open questions should trigger a `needs discussion` signal in the planning surface. When a question is marked answered, RepoPlanner may create a new decision entry in the same non-AI inline composer flow and then backfill the linked decision reference. |
| **Rationale** | Agent uncertainty should be captured in planning memory instead of disappearing into chat logs, while answered questions should stay reviewable without crowding the main surface. A small number of questions is normal; multiple open questions indicate higher planning entropy and a better candidate for human discussion than random implementation pickup. The question workflow is plain UI support, not an in-product AI planning feature. |
| **Consequences** | Tracked by `REPOPLAN-REQ-23`, `repo-planner-integration-05-05`, `repo-planner-integration-05-06`, and `repo-planner-integration-06-08`. |

## `REPOPLAN-ROOTS-01` -- Config-declared planning directories, not implicit doc-tree scans

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| **Decision** | CLI snapshot, scoring, and bundle builders resolve planning inputs **only** from **`planning-config.toml`** (or successor) declarations: one or more **workspace roots**, optional **descendant discovery** under a parent, and a **planning directory basename** per root (default **`.planning`**, with support for names like **`planning`**). The conventional monorepo case remains a single repo-root **`.planning/`** as the global machine loop; **global** may be remapped to another path. The tool does **not** crawl arbitrary subtrees (e.g. all MDX under `content/docs`) unless a root + discovery rule explicitly includes them. |
| **Rationale** | Predictable performance, correct multi-root monorepos, and no accidental coupling to one site’s docs layout. |
| **Consequences** | Tracked by `REPOPLAN-REQ-24`, `repo-planner-integration-03-03`, `repo-planner-integration-05-01`, and future `planning-config.toml` schema work. Live GET routes should use the same resolver as snapshot/scoring instead of hard-coding `.planning` in each handler. |

## Open (resolve in task registry)

No unresolved phase-03 cockpit data-plane items remain. Open workflow and structured-editing questions now live under `repo-planner-integration-05` and `repo-planner-integration-06`.

## Minimal init -- what went wrong once

An early **`--minimal`** pass still wrote **`REQUIREMENTS.md`** / **`IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`** *inside* **`.planning/`**, breaking the bare tree. **`usage.jsonl`** also recreated **`.planning/reports/`**. **Fixes:** **`runPlanningInitMinimal`**, **`REPOPLANNER_REPORTS_DIR`**, **`scripts/run-planning-cli.mjs`** defaulting reports to **`.planning-reports/`** -- full table in [getting started](/docs/repo-planner/getting-started) and [decisions](/docs/repo-planner/planning/decisions) **`REPOPLAN-REPORTS-01`**.

## Workflow stance

- **Roadmap, state, task registry, and decisions** are the living records.
- Any one-off phase plan should live under a section planning folder such as `planning/plans/<phase-id>/`.
- `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` should not exist in the minimal root tree; if an older clone still has it, migrate the useful content into roadmap/state/task-registry/decisions or section plans and delete the file.
- RepoPlanner should align with the same structure instead of encouraging parallel "plan vs task registry" workflows.

## Host embed on this site

**Intent:** Browser edits stay **local / export → git**; the server does not persist casual cockpit mutations. Full detail: [Decisions](/docs/repo-planner/planning/decisions) **`REPOPLAN-EMBED-01`**, **`REPOPLAN-SHELL-01`**, **`REPOPLAN-MODES-01`**.

**Modes:** **Pack** (gallery + uploads; client-side bundle where possible) vs **Live** (GET APIs reading this repo’s **`.planning/`**). That mode split, toolbar shell, init download flow, and contrast pass are now shipped in phase `repo-planner-integration-03`; workflow support is now shipped in phase `repo-planner-integration-05`, and follow-on work moves into structured editing (`06`).

| Surface | Default behavior |
| --- | --- |
| **GET** planning APIs | Read committed planning files through the shared config/root resolver (`planning-config.toml` roots; repo-root **`.planning/`** fallback only when config does not declare roots). Live bundle JSON is built directly from XML / planning files rather than CLI spawn per poll |
| **`POST /api/planning-edits/apply`**, **`POST /api/planning-cli/run`** | **403** unless **`PLANNING_COCKPIT_ALLOW_SERVER_WRITES=1`** |
| **Planning chat / in-site LLM** | **Out of scope** -- **`POST /api/ai/planning-chat`** returns **501** unless you wire a backend |
| **Workspace chrome** (project switcher, pack upload, export, init downloads) | Owned by the **`repo-planner`** package and imported; portfolio mounts the shell and supplies routes/env (**`REPOPLAN-SHELL-01`**). Live and Pack now share the same vendored **`PlanningCockpit`** body through the shared data-source contract rather than keeping a host-only fallback pane. |

## Workflow support shipped

- Snapshot ranking, current-sprint context, and the compact `AGENTS.md` reminder now come from one shared workflow engine across CLI, Live, and Pack.
- `workflow kickoff`, `workflow done-gate`, and `workflow ownership` now expose the same phase steering data the cockpit uses.
- Kickoff output now includes a reusable block template, with a committed example at `planning/plans/repo-planner-integration-05/PLAN.mdx`.
- Executable work cannot be marked done through the CLI without tests on the verification path.
- The cockpit dashboard now surfaces workflow cards for recommendation, kickoff state, done-gate warnings, ownership guidance, open questions, and collapsed answered-question history.
- The Pack overview tab now surfaces workflow summary cards and host-policy badges instead of only raw file KPIs.
- The inspector now renders structured requirement, roadmap, state, decisions, kickoff, task-registry, and `-PLAN.xml` phase-plan views with host-policy badges and locked-id guidance rather than defaulting to raw markdown/XML.
- Phase-question answers now use a non-AI inline composer that can create a decision record, backfill the linked reference, and keep answered history collapsed in the same inspector surface.
- Advanced id migration now exists only for writable local packs and rewrites stable string-id references across pack files; Live mode and short numeric/root ids stay locked.

### Data contract (conceptual)

Vendor UI expects a **bundle** (see `Bundle` in `planning-cockpit.tsx`): `snapshot`, `openTasks`, `openQuestions`, `context.summary.phases`, `agentsWithTasks`, etc. Host implements something like **`CockpitDataSource`**: `getBundle()`, `getMetrics()`, `getLatestReport()`, polling policy.

| Source | `getBundle()` | `getMetrics()` | Report markdown |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Live monorepo** | `/api/planning-state` (today: CLI JSON) → later XML reader route | `/api/planning-metrics` | `/api/planning-reports/latest` |
| **Uploaded pack** | Client or **`POST /api/planning-pack/parse`** → same JSON shape | Synthetic series or honest empty | First `*.md` in pack or summary from XML |

**Resolved in phase `03`:** Pack mode now builds a cockpit bundle from XML/markdown planning files and exposes honest single-point metrics with no fake historical series.

### Phase `repo-planner-integration-06` notes

- Forms should support visible phase open questions even when they do not block execution.
- Questions use a single text field.
- Question states are phase-only and limited to `open` and `answered`; answered items stay visible in a collapsed history area.
- Questions stay in created order.
- Each open question contributes equally to entropy. Two or more open questions should surface a `needs discussion` signal that lowers implementation recommendation and changes the suggested action toward discussion.
- Raw source stays hidden in RepoPlanner's normal product surface.
- Phase-question editing now ships on `-PLAN.xml` inspectors, and the pack-only migration flow is intentionally limited to stable string ids rather than short numeric/root ids.

## Registry

| Field | Value |

## Core Requirements

| Id | Requirement | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-01` | RepoPlanner should align with the repo's roadmap-first planning loop instead of reviving standalone implementation-plan drift | the tool needs to reinforce the planning model actually used in this repo |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-02` | the public portfolio embed should remain read-only by default and avoid casual server-side repo writes | operators can export and commit locally without turning the site into a write surface |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-03` | the data plane should understand root `.planning/*.xml` and section planning packs with one consistent bundle shape | live repo mode and uploaded packs should feed the same cockpit model |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-04` | RepoPlanner work in this repo should stay tied to the vendored submodule and documented host contracts | upstream changes and host glue need a clear ownership path |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-05` | CLI productization should treat RepoPlanner and `repub` as separate tools even when both are documented from the same monorepo | the commands solve different problems and should not collapse into one mega-CLI |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-06` | RepoPlanner should help agents pick a phase quickly by surfacing active phases, percent complete, estimated effort, dependencies, and missing verification data | agents should not need to reread the whole planning tree just to choose the next good phase |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-07` | RepoPlanner should scaffold kickoff and closeout flows around the planning records when appropriate (**vague**, **stale**, or **estimated hours above policy cutoff** — see [decisions](/docs/repo-planner/planning/decisions) **`REPOPLAN-KICKOFF-01`**), instead of requiring kickoff for every phase or encouraging freeform drift | the tool should reinforce the roadmap/state/task-registry/decisions loop already used in this repo |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-08` | executable behavior cannot be marked done in RepoPlanner workflows unless automated tests are present in the phase verification path | the UI and CLI should enforce the repo's hard rule for definition of done |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-09` | RepoPlanner should make global-vs-section ownership legible when agents create or pick phases | the tool should help users place work correctly, not just edit files faster |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-10` | The embed must treat **Pack** and **Live** as explicit modes: Pack uses built-in or uploaded pack data; Live uses server APIs that read the mounted repo | operators should never confuse local pack data with the server's `.planning/` tree |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-11` | Cockpit chrome should follow a dashboard layout: persistent left file tree at the primary breakpoint, top toolbar for import and grouped export actions, and labels that change with mode | matches the shell the portfolio is converging on |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-12` | Parsing and bundle shape for XML/MD/MDX in the embed should stay aligned with the same rules as `loop-cli` and shared `planning-parse-core` so cockpit and CLI disagree only when data differs, not when parsers diverge | one behavioral contract for planning files |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-13` | Snapshot should prioritize structured steering data over raw document text: ranked active/planned phases, blocking warnings, ownership hints, progress, missing DoD fields, missing tests, stale pointers, orphan tasks, and current sprint status | operators need help choosing and closing work, not a second document reader |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-14` | `AGENTS.md` should appear in RepoPlanner as a condensed workflow reminder and deep link, not as a fully inlined document inside the snapshot | the tool should reinforce the loop without drowning the operator in prose |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-15` | Planning records should be editable through form-based structured inputs wherever the schema is known: phase metadata, task rows, DoD fields, verification, status, dependencies, ownership, sprint membership, and phase open questions | the cockpit should feel like a planner, not a raw markdown/xml editor |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-16` | File tree remains the source-of-truth navigation metaphor, but the main pane should render parsed data as cards, lists, tables, badges, sprint views, and percentages instead of page-like prose by default | planning files are storage and navigation units; workflow decisions need higher-level views |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-17` | Visual semantics should stay consistent across CLI, cockpit, and docs: shared status colors, badges, icons, warning hierarchy, and effort labels | agents need to recognize urgency, risk, and ownership quickly |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-18` | Repo-specific workflow rules must be configurable host policy, while RepoPlanner stays a reusable product that can support looser or different planning contracts outside this portfolio repo | this monorepo helps shape the product, but should not hard-code every portfolio rule into upstream defaults |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-19` | Recommendation scoring should favor strategic importance, boost executable phases that still need tests, apply a shrinking penalty to Global work, treat many unresolved phase questions as planning entropy, use weighted task effort, and use created/updated timestamps only as tie-breakers or small boosts | ranking should match how work is actually chosen in this repo |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-20` | Sprint support should be first-class in snapshot and forms: open active/planned phases only, grouped by current sprint, with sprint defined automatically from phase ordering unless host policy says otherwise | the tool should show planning windows, not just isolated rows |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-21` | IDs should be immutable in the normal editing flow after creation; any rename must be an advanced migration action that rewrites references and is unavailable in read-only Live mode | ids are references, not casual labels |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-22` | Pack or local editing flows should autosave client-side with debounced writes and visible save-state feedback; Live mode remains read-only and never implies persistent browser edits to the mounted repo | operators want low-friction editing without confusing it for live repo mutation |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-23` | RepoPlanner should support visible open questions per phase so agents can record what they want answered without blocking work; questions remain phase-scoped, stay inspectable, use a single text field, use only `open` and `answered` (decision recorded) states, contribute equally to planning entropy, and stay in created order | planning should capture agent uncertainty instead of hiding it in chat history |
| `REPOPLAN-REQ-24` | Snapshot and scoring must read only planning trees declared in config. Conventionally the global root points at the repo's primary machine loop; each root lists a directory name to treat as the planning folder. Config may define multiple roots and descendant discovery rules | monorepos often have one root `.planning` plus section planners; discovery rules belong in config, not implicit magic |

## Snapshot Contract

| Area | Display |
| --- | --- |
| `phase recommendation` | ranked list of active/planned phases with score, why-now reasons, blockers, ownership target, sprint, and direct links to roadmap/state/task rows |
| `workflow reminder` | compact AGENTS summary card: read order, **when kickoff is required** (vague / stale / **estimated hours** over policy max), tests-required-for-done rule, and a deep link to the full file |
| `risk lane` | badges for missing tests, missing DoD, blocked dependencies, stale phases, orphan tasks, stale state pointers, and unresolved phase open questions |
| `progress lane` | completion percent, open task counts, phase age, recent activity, effort label, and sprint progress |
| `ownership lane` | whether work belongs in Global, a section, or both, with the file targets that should be edited |
| `detail pane` | structured lists, forms, percentages, sprint views, and task tables by default; raw file text stays hidden in normal RepoPlanner flows |

## Section Boundaries

| Topic | Rule |
| --- | --- |
| `submodule` | implementation changes land in `vendor/repo-planner`, then this repo consumes the bump |
| `embed` | portfolio owns host layout and routes; long-term cockpit chrome belongs in the package |
| `planning` | use section-local roadmap, state, task registry, decisions, and errors-and-attempts for RepoPlanner integration work |
| `root-loop` | root `.planning/*.xml` remains the machine-readable monorepo layer that RepoPlanner reads and visualizes |
| `global-entrypoint` | Global docs remain the human-first workflow surface; RepoPlanner should summarize and accelerate that loop, not replace its rules |
| `host-policy` | portfolio-specific hard rules such as tests-required-for-done must be configurable policy flags or adapters, not invisible upstream assumptions |
